condition: Hereditary hemochromatosis (HFE C282Y)

elevated: Homozygous C282Y — high risk for iron overload
Monitor ferritin and transferrin saturation regularly
Consider genetic counseling
carrier: Heterozygous C282Y carrier — low risk alone
Monitor ferritin if compound heterozygous with H63D

condition: Hereditary hemochromatosis (HFE H63D)

moderate: Homozygous H63D — moderate iron overload risk
Higher risk if also C282Y carrier (compound heterozygote)
carrier: Check C282Y status for compound heterozygosity

condition: MTHFR C677T — reduced folate metabolism

elevated: Homozygous C677T — ~70% reduced MTHFR enzyme activity
Consider methylfolate supplementation over folic acid
Monitor homocysteine levels
moderate: Heterozygous C677T — ~35% reduced MTHFR activity
Monitor homocysteine, especially if B12/folate low

condition: MTHFR A1298C — mildly reduced folate metabolism

moderate: Homozygous A1298C — mild MTHFR reduction
Greater impact if also C677T heterozygous (compound)

condition: APOE e4 — cardiovascular and Alzheimer risk

elevated: APOE e4/e4 homozygous — significantly elevated Alzheimer risk
Higher LDL cholesterol response to dietary fat
Consider aggressive cardiovascular risk management
moderate: APOE e4 heterozygous — moderately elevated risk
Monitor lipid panel closely

condition: Factor V Leiden — thrombophilia

elevated: Homozygous Factor V Leiden — high VTE risk
Avoid oral contraceptives, discuss anticoagulation
DVT/PE risk significantly elevated
moderate: Heterozygous Factor V Leiden — moderate VTE risk
Discuss with hematologist before surgery or immobilization

condition: Slow caffeine metabolism

moderate: Slow caffeine metabolizer — caffeine stays in system longer
Higher cardiovascular risk from heavy coffee consumption
Consider limiting caffeine intake

condition: VDR FokI — altered vitamin D receptor

moderate: VDR variant — may need higher vitamin D levels for same effect
Monitor 25-OH vitamin D more closely
carrier: VDR heterozygous — minor impact on vitamin D signaling

condition: CYP2D6 poor metabolizer

elevated: CYP2D6 poor metabolizer — many drugs metabolized slowly
Affects: codeine, tramadol, tamoxifen, some antidepressants
Codeine will NOT convert to morphine — ineffective for pain
moderate: CYP2D6 intermediate metabolizer — some drugs affected
Discuss with pharmacist before starting CYP2D6 substrates

condition: CYP2C19 poor metabolizer

elevated: CYP2C19 poor metabolizer — clopidogrel (Plavix) may be ineffective
Also affects PPIs, some antidepressants, and antifungals
Consider alternative antiplatelet if prescribed clopidogrel
moderate: CYP2C19 intermediate metabolizer — reduced clopidogrel activation

condition: Lactose intolerance (genetic predisposition)

moderate: Genetically predisposed to lactose intolerance
Lactase production typically declines in adulthood
Consider lactase supplements or dairy alternatives

condition: CYP2C9 reduced metabolism — warfarin sensitivity

elevated: Homozygous CYP2C9*2 — significantly reduced warfarin metabolism
Requires substantial warfarin dose reduction (per CPIC guidelines)
Higher bleeding risk at standard doses — monitor INR closely
moderate: Heterozygous CYP2C9*2 — moderately reduced warfarin metabolism
May require ~20% warfarin dose reduction per CPIC

condition: CYP2C9 poor metabolism — warfarin sensitivity, NSAID metabolism

elevated: Homozygous CYP2C9*3 — severely reduced CYP2C9 activity (~5% residual)
Major warfarin dose reduction required per CPIC guidelines
Prolonged NSAID exposure — increased GI bleeding risk
moderate: Heterozygous CYP2C9*3 — reduced warfarin and NSAID metabolism
Warfarin dose adjustment recommended per CPIC

condition: VKORC1 variant — reduced warfarin dose requirement

elevated: Homozygous VKORC1 -1639A — low-dose warfarin required
Standard doses cause supratherapeutic INR and bleeding risk
CPIC recommends 50-60% dose reduction from standard
moderate: Heterozygous VKORC1 -1639G>A — intermediate warfarin sensitivity
CPIC recommends 25-50% dose reduction from standard

condition: SLCO1B1 reduced transport — statin myopathy risk

elevated: Homozygous SLCO1B1*5 — high risk for statin-induced myopathy
Avoid simvastatin >20mg; consider rosuvastatin or pravastatin (CPIC)
Monitor CK levels if on any statin therapy
moderate: Heterozygous SLCO1B1*5 — moderate statin myopathy risk
Avoid high-dose simvastatin; lower-risk statins preferred

condition: DPYD deficiency — fluoropyrimidine toxicity risk

elevated: Homozygous DPYD*2A — complete DPD deficiency
Fluoropyrimidines (5-FU, capecitabine) are CONTRAINDICATED (CPIC)
Life-threatening toxicity risk — use alternative chemotherapy
moderate: Heterozygous DPYD*2A — partial DPD deficiency
Reduce fluoropyrimidine dose by at least 50% per CPIC guidelines

condition: UGT1A1*28 — Gilbert syndrome, irinotecan toxicity risk

elevated: Homozygous UGT1A1*28 (7/7 TA repeats) — Gilbert syndrome
Elevated unconjugated bilirubin; usually benign
Irinotecan dose reduction required — risk of severe neutropenia (CPIC)
moderate: Heterozygous UGT1A1*28 (6/7) — mildly reduced glucuronidation
Possible mild bilirubin elevation; intermediate irinotecan toxicity risk

condition: NAT2 slow acetylator — isoniazid toxicity risk

elevated: Homozygous NAT2 slow acetylator — high risk for isoniazid hepatotoxicity
Slow acetylation of aromatic amines and hydrazine drugs
Monitor liver function closely on isoniazid, sulfasalazine, or dapsone
moderate: Heterozygous NAT2 — intermediate acetylator
Moderate isoniazid hepatotoxicity risk; monitor ALT/AST

condition: TPMT deficiency — thiopurine toxicity risk

elevated: Homozygous TPMT*3B — negligible TPMT activity
Thiopurines (azathioprine, 6-MP, thioguanine) at standard doses cause severe myelosuppression
CPIC: reduce dose to ~10% of standard or use alternative agent
moderate: Heterozygous TPMT*3B — intermediate TPMT activity
CPIC: reduce thiopurine dose by 30-70% and monitor CBC closely

condition: CYP3A4 reduced activity — altered drug metabolism

elevated: Homozygous CYP3A4*22 — significantly reduced CYP3A4 expression
Affects metabolism of tacrolimus, statins, many chemotherapeutics
Lower starting doses recommended for CYP3A4 substrates
moderate: Heterozygous CYP3A4*22 — moderately reduced CYP3A4 activity
Consider dose adjustments for narrow therapeutic index CYP3A4 substrates

condition: COMT Val158Met — slow catecholamine metabolism

elevated: Homozygous Met/Met — 3-4x reduced COMT enzyme activity
Slower dopamine/norepinephrine/estrogen catechol clearance
May benefit from reduced methyl donor supplementation; sensitive to stress
moderate: Heterozygous Val/Met — intermediate COMT activity
Moderate impact on catecholamine and estrogen catechol metabolism

condition: MTR A2756G — altered methionine synthase activity

elevated: Homozygous MTR 2756GG — upregulated methionine synthase
Increased B12 utilization; may deplete B12 stores faster
Monitor B12 and homocysteine levels regularly
moderate: Heterozygous MTR A2756G — mildly increased B12 demand
Ensure adequate B12 intake, especially if vegetarian/vegan

condition: MTRR A66G — reduced methionine synthase reductase

elevated: Homozygous MTRR 66GG — reduced regeneration of methylcobalamin
Impaired B12 recycling may elevate homocysteine
Combined risk with MTHFR variants; ensure adequate B12 and folate
moderate: Heterozygous MTRR A66G — mildly reduced B12 recycling
Monitor homocysteine if combined with other methylation variants

condition: CBS C699T — upregulated transsulfuration pathway

elevated: Homozygous CBS C699T — upregulated CBS enzyme activity
Faster homocysteine-to-cystathionine conversion; may lower homocysteine
Increased ammonia and sulfite production; may deplete B6 faster
moderate: Heterozygous CBS C699T — mildly upregulated transsulfuration
Monitor homocysteine and B6 status in context of other methylation variants

condition: BHMT R239Q — altered betaine-homocysteine methyltransferase

elevated: Homozygous BHMT R239Q — reduced betaine-dependent homocysteine remethylation
May contribute to elevated homocysteine via the betaine shortcut pathway
Consider betaine (TMG) supplementation and monitor homocysteine
moderate: Heterozygous BHMT R239Q — mildly reduced betaine pathway activity
Monitor homocysteine if combined with MTHFR or MTR variants

condition: LPA variant — elevated lipoprotein(a), CVD risk

elevated: Homozygous LPA risk allele — substantially elevated Lp(a) levels
Strong independent risk factor for atherosclerotic CVD and aortic stenosis
No approved Lp(a)-lowering therapy; aggressive LDL management recommended
moderate: Heterozygous LPA variant — moderately elevated Lp(a)
Measure Lp(a) level directly; optimize other cardiovascular risk factors

condition: PCSK9 R46L — loss-of-function, lower LDL, cardioprotective

protective: PCSK9 R46L loss-of-function variant — lifelong lower LDL cholesterol
Associated with ~30-40% reduction in coronary heart disease risk
Phenocopied by PCSK9 inhibitor medications (evolocumab, alirocumab)

condition: 9p21.3 variant — coronary artery disease risk

elevated: Homozygous 9p21.3 risk allele — ~1.6x increased CAD risk
One of the strongest common genetic risk factors for coronary artery disease
Aggressive lipid management and cardiovascular prevention recommended
moderate: Heterozygous 9p21.3 risk allele — ~1.3x increased CAD risk
Optimize modifiable risk factors: lipids, blood pressure, smoking, exercise

condition: CETP TaqIB — altered HDL metabolism

elevated: Homozygous CETP TaqIB B2/B2 — reduced CETP activity, higher HDL
Higher HDL levels but clinical CVD benefit is context-dependent
Interpret HDL values with genetic context; focus on LDL and triglycerides
moderate: Heterozygous CETP TaqIB — moderately reduced CETP activity
May have higher HDL; evaluate full lipid panel for cardiovascular risk

condition: ACE insertion/deletion — ACE activity and hypertension risk

elevated: Homozygous ACE D/D — elevated ACE levels and angiotensin II activity
Associated with increased hypertension and left ventricular hypertrophy risk
May have enhanced response to ACE inhibitors
moderate: Heterozygous ACE I/D — intermediate ACE levels
Monitor blood pressure regularly; consider ACE inhibitor response

condition: AGT M235T — elevated angiotensinogen, hypertension risk

elevated: Homozygous AGT 235T — elevated plasma angiotensinogen levels
Associated with increased essential hypertension and preeclampsia risk
Sodium-sensitive blood pressure response; consider dietary sodium restriction
moderate: Heterozygous AGT M235T — moderately elevated angiotensinogen
Monitor blood pressure; dietary sodium moderation advised

condition: NOS3 Glu298Asp — reduced endothelial nitric oxide

elevated: Homozygous NOS3 298Asp — reduced endothelial nitric oxide production
Associated with endothelial dysfunction, hypertension, and coronary spasm
May benefit from L-arginine, L-citrulline, or nitrate-rich diet
moderate: Heterozygous NOS3 Glu298Asp — mildly reduced NO bioavailability
Monitor blood pressure and cardiovascular risk factors

condition: TNF-alpha -308G>A — enhanced inflammatory response

elevated: Homozygous TNF-alpha -308A — significantly elevated TNF-alpha production
Associated with increased susceptibility to autoimmune and inflammatory conditions
Monitor inflammatory markers; anti-inflammatory dietary strategies may help
moderate: Heterozygous TNF-alpha -308G>A — moderately elevated TNF-alpha
Monitor CRP and inflammatory markers during illness or flares

condition: IL-6 -174G>C — altered inflammatory cytokine production

elevated: Homozygous IL-6 -174CC — altered IL-6 regulation with age-dependent effects
Associated with higher baseline inflammatory state in older adults
Monitor hs-CRP; omega-3 and anti-inflammatory strategies recommended
moderate: Heterozygous IL-6 -174G>C — intermediate IL-6 production
Context-dependent inflammatory risk; monitor hs-CRP as baseline

condition: HLA-DQ2.5 — celiac disease genetic susceptibility

elevated: Homozygous HLA-DQ2.5 — highest genetic risk for celiac disease
~95% of celiac patients carry HLA-DQ2; necessary but not sufficient
Screen with tTG-IgA if symptomatic; do NOT start gluten-free diet before testing
moderate: Heterozygous HLA-DQ2.5 — celiac disease susceptibility present
Consider tTG-IgA screening if GI symptoms, iron deficiency, or family history

condition: HLA-DQ8 — celiac disease and type 1 diabetes risk

elevated: Homozygous HLA-DQ8 — elevated risk for celiac disease and type 1 diabetes
Present in ~5-10% of celiac patients who are DQ2-negative
Screen with tTG-IgA and monitor glucose/HbA1c for T1D
moderate: Heterozygous HLA-DQ8 — genetic susceptibility to celiac and T1D
Monitor for celiac symptoms and glycemic markers

condition: FTO variant — obesity risk and appetite regulation

elevated: Homozygous FTO risk allele — ~1.7x increased obesity risk
Associated with increased hunger, reduced satiety signaling, and higher BMI
Responds well to structured physical activity — exercise attenuates genetic effect
moderate: Heterozygous FTO variant — ~1.3x increased obesity risk
Moderate impact on appetite regulation; lifestyle interventions effective

condition: FADS1 variant — altered omega-3/6 fatty acid conversion

elevated: Homozygous FADS1 low-activity allele — reduced delta-5 desaturase activity
Impaired conversion of ALA to EPA/DHA and LA to arachidonic acid
May require preformed EPA/DHA supplementation (fish oil or algal oil)
moderate: Heterozygous FADS1 variant — moderately reduced fatty acid conversion
Consider increasing dietary EPA/DHA from marine sources

condition: BCMO1 variant — reduced beta-carotene to vitamin A conversion

elevated: Homozygous BCMO1 379V — ~70% reduced beta-carotene cleavage activity
Plant-based beta-carotene is poorly converted to retinol
Preformed vitamin A (retinol) from animal sources or supplements recommended
moderate: Heterozygous BCMO1 A379V — ~30-40% reduced beta-carotene conversion
Include both preformed retinol and beta-carotene dietary sources

condition: SOD2 Ala16Val — altered mitochondrial antioxidant defense

elevated: Homozygous SOD2 16Val — reduced mitochondrial superoxide dismutase activity
Increased mitochondrial oxidative stress and ROS accumulation
Ensure adequate manganese, CoQ10, and antioxidant intake
moderate: Heterozygous SOD2 Ala16Val — moderately reduced MnSOD activity
Support antioxidant defenses; avoid excessive iron supplementation

condition: GPX1 Pro198Leu — reduced glutathione peroxidase activity

elevated: Homozygous GPX1 198Leu — significantly reduced glutathione peroxidase 1
Impaired hydrogen peroxide detoxification; increased oxidative damage risk
Ensure adequate selenium intake — GPX1 is selenium-dependent
moderate: Heterozygous GPX1 Pro198Leu — moderately reduced GPX1 activity
Optimize selenium status; support glutathione production

condition: GSTP1 Ile105Val — altered phase II detoxification

elevated: Homozygous GSTP1 105Val — altered substrate specificity for glutathione conjugation
Reduced detoxification of some carcinogens and environmental toxins
Support glutathione with cruciferous vegetables, NAC, and glycine
moderate: Heterozygous GSTP1 Ile105Val — intermediate phase II detox capacity
Support phase II detoxification with sulfur-rich foods and antioxidants

condition: ALDH2*2 — acetaldehyde accumulation, alcohol intolerance

elevated: Homozygous ALDH2*2 — near-complete loss of aldehyde dehydrogenase 2 activity
Severe alcohol intolerance (flushing, nausea, tachycardia)
Significantly elevated esophageal cancer risk with any alcohol consumption
moderate: Heterozygous ALDH2*2 — reduced acetaldehyde clearance (Asian flush)
Increased esophageal and head/neck cancer risk with alcohol use

condition: ADH1B*2 — rapid alcohol to acetaldehyde conversion

elevated: Homozygous ADH1B*2 — ~40x faster ethanol-to-acetaldehyde conversion
Rapid acetaldehyde accumulation causes flushing and discomfort
Protective against alcoholism but increases acetaldehyde exposure per drink
moderate: Heterozygous ADH1B*2 — faster alcohol oxidation than typical
Reduced alcoholism risk; higher acetaldehyde exposure per drink
